title: craspedia minor var. viscosa
intro: Craspedia minor var. viscosa, also known as Sticky Bottlebrush, is a small shrubby plant with yellow or yellow-green flowers, native to Australia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its essential oils and flavonoids. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Craspedia minor var. viscosa can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or extracts, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
